response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack/web engineer with 14+ years of experience and some genuine low-latency systems work, but the resume shows essentially no overlap with this role's core requirements. There is no SoC or IP modeling, no AI accelerator or GPU architecture experience, no deep-learning workload performance analysis, and no hardware-software co-design at any level, let alone principal. The experience bar (BS + 8+ years) is met, but the domain mismatch is near-total.
strengths: 14+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second)
Some C/C++ systems background (NI LabWindows/CVI components, wave-field synthesis audio thesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon performance modeling, or system-on-chip architecture experience whatsoever
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web and product engineering, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Domain mismatch with SoC/AI accelerator architecture
No deep-learning workload or GPU performance experience
Low-latency systems and performance tuning experience
